description: 'lori' has two closely related uses: continued life may depend on something, or a living being may show vigorous energy. Water is vital to a garden; a person can look vital after recovery. 'noetha' (essential) covers necessity in any task, while 'kenua' (energy) is the capacity for action rather than its living expression.
articulatory_notes: Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for voiced lateral 'l' and release into stressed true-mid back rounded 'o'. Make a tap or short trill at the same ridge, then raise the front of the tongue for high-front unrounded 'i'.
sound_symbolism: 'lori' echoes the liquid movement and vowels of 'lioru' (life) without matching it exactly. Across the tap, rounded 'o' rises towards narrow unrounded 'i'. The quick upward turn suits its vigorous sense.

examples.translation: Water is vital to the tree.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Water and functioning ecosystems are 'lori' when continued life depends on them. The adjective keeps life at the centre of the claim.

semantic_domains.nature: conditions and relations on which living systems depend
semantic_domains.physical: vigorous energy expressed by a living body
